'.] डॉ. बी. बी. lसंह, उप महािनदेशक MINISTRY OF STATISTICS AND PROGRAMME IMPLEMENTATION NOTIFICATION New Delhi, the 21st November, 2017 S.O. 3699(E).—In exercise of the powers conferred by sections 3 and 4 of the Collection of Statistics Act, 2008 and rules 5 and 7 of the Collection of Statistics Rules, 2011, the Ministry of Statistics & Programme Implementation hereby directs Collection of Statistics on manufacturing and related activities through a statistical survey, hereinafter referred to as ‘Annual Survey of Industries 2016-17’, as per the details mentioned in the Schedule-I. 2. The Ministry further appoints the persons as also their assignees in office, mentioned in the Schedule II as statistics officers in respect of the geographical units mentioned thereof with reference to the aforementioned direction. Each statistics officer shall immediately submit an undertaking in the prescribed format, namely, Form-I appended to the Collection of Statistics Rules, 2011, to the Additional Director General, Field Operations Division, National Sample Survey Office, New Delhi. 3. The persons authorized by each statistics officer, after submission of undertaking in the prescribed format, namely, Form-I appended to the Collection of Statistics Rules, 2011 to the statistics officer, would be engaged in the geographical jurisdiction of the statistics officer for verification of information furnished by informants, for inspecting relevant records, and for seeking clarifications, as may be necessary, from informants. In accordance with these duties, each person authorized would carry a photo identity card and a letter of authorization issued by the concerned statistics officer. Statistics officer shall keep the undertakings of the authorized persons in his safe custody and forward a certificate of having obtained the above undertakings to the Office of the Additional Director General, Field Operations Division, National Sample Survey Office, New Delhi. 4. The statistics collected with reference to the aforementioned direction will be processed by officials working at the Office of the Deputy Director General, Industrial Statistics Wing, National Sample Survey Office, Kolkata of this Ministry. Subject to the provisions of the Collection of Statistics Act, 2008 and the Collection of Statistics Rules, 2011, the unit level data and the reports, generated from the data through statistical techniques, may be kept in custody and disseminated by the Industrial Statistics Wing and the Computer Centre of this Ministry and the Labour Bureau under the Ministry of Labour and Employment to the users within and outside the Government, the States/UTs and the public at large as per policy guidelines of the Government. -- 5 of 8 -- 6 THE GAZETTE OF INDIA : EXTRAORDINARY [PART II—SEC. 3(ii)] 5. All persons engaged in any activity in respect of collection of statistics, processing of data and dissemination with respect to Annual Survey of Industries 2016-17 are governed by the provisions under the Collection of Statistics Act, 2008 and the Collection of Statistics Rules, 2011. 6. Following powers have been delegated to the statistics officers: (a) A statistics officer is empowered to depute/authorize officials to collect statistics in respect of the aforementioned direction, to verify any information furnished by informants on the basis of business records and other records and to seek clarifications from any owner or occupier or person in-charge of a factory or establishment or management, as the case may be, at the premises where such records are kept. A statistics officer is also empowered to depute/authorize officials under the administrative control of any State Government or Union territory Administration, which is willing to collect statistics from any of those factories/establishment for which the statistics officer has not deputed/authorized any official under his administrative control. (b) A statistics officer is empowered to give one month notice or for the period deemed fit to each of the informant for furnishing return in the format, specified for the purpose of collection of statistics. (c) A statistics officer is empowered to provide to any informant, the facility of furnishing return in electronic form through a web-portal for certain period, to be specified by him in the notice to be issued to the informant. SCHEDULE-I Subject and purpose for collection of statistics: 1. Statistics relating to growth, composition and structure of organized manufacturing sector comprising activities related to manufacturing processes, repair services, gas and water supply and cold storage is collected through Annual Survey of Industries 2016-17. Geographical area for collection of statistics: 2. The Annual Survey of Industries 2016-17 is conducted in the whole of India, including the State of Jammu and Kashmir. In case of the State of Jammu and Kashmir, the provisions under the Collection of Statistics Act, 2008 will apply in respect of collection of statistics on matters covered under the Union List and the Concurrent List, as applicable to the State, in accordance with the provisions under the Collection of Statistics (Amendment) Act, 2017 which has been brought into force on 3rd October, 2017. Method of data collection: 3. A Notice will be issued by each statistics officer to informants under his jurisdiction, indicating therein the date by which, the officer or office to whom, the unit or units for which, and the formats and the manner in which information is required to be furnished, including submission of information in electronic form through a web-portal designed for the purpose and made available to informants up to a certain date, specified in the notice. Nature of informants for whom data may be collected: 4. The informants are owners or occupiers or persons in-charge, of factories registered under the Factories Act, 1948 or of establishments registered under the Beedi & Cigar Workers (Conditions of Employment) Act,1966 or the units registered under any of the following Acts/Boards/Authority viz., Companies Act, 1956, Shops and Commercial Establishments Act, Societies Registration Act, Cooperative Societies Act, Khadi and Village Industries Board, Directorate of Industries(District industries centre) who would be issued notice by statistics officer for furnishing information under the aforementioned direction. 5. A statistics officer may require furnishing of consolidated information in respect of two or more factories or establishments under a single management operating within a State or Union territory, in case information is not separately available for individual factories or establishments, through a notice issued to the concerned owner or occupier or person in-charge of the management. Period during which collection of statistics may be completed: 6. The date for submission of information by each informant would be mentioned in the notice and would generally fall during November 2017 to June 2018. -- 6 of 8 -- ¹Hkkx IIμ[k.M 3(ii)º Hkkjr dk jkti=k % vlk/kj.k 7 Reference period: 7. Information is required to be furnished for the financial year commencing from 1st April 2016 and ending on 31st March 2017 or for the Accounting Year of a unit ending on any date between 1st April 2016 and 31st March 2017. Nature of information to be collected: 8. The information required relates to assets and liabilities, receipts, expenses, input items (indigenous and imported), products and by-products, distributive expenses, employment and labour cost, working days, man-days worked, absenteeism, labour turnover, man-hours worked, earning and social security benefits. Language in which information is to be furnished by informant: 9. An informant shall furnish information in the prescribed formats, either in Hindi or in English. Obligation of informant: 10. An owner or occupier or person in-charge of a factory or establishment or management, as the case may be, shall furnish information in the manner and by the date mentioned in the notice received by him from the concerned statistics officer. He should also furnish relevant records for inspection, and answer questions in relation to the information sought, as may be required by the statistics officer or a person authorized by him. Nature of business records and other records which may be inspected: 11. Business records of a unit, such as balance sheet, profit and loss account, muster rolls, attendance registers, labour register, pay rolls, Director’s report or any other document in support of the information furnished may be inspected by the statistics officer or a person authorized by him. The manner of inspection: 12. The concerned statistics officer or a person authorized by him may verify the information furnished under the aforementioned direction on the basis of business records and other records of the unit and seek clarifications, at the premises where such records are kept, from the concerned owner or occupier or a person in-charge of a factory or establishment or management, as the case may be, or a person authorized by him of the unit.
